rationale: The cited paper is not just listed in background citations; it is used to position the focal channel-interaction question and to motivate heterogeneity by prior brand presence. The citing paper explicitly states that it follows Wang and Goldfarb (2017)'s methodology to estimate how treatment effects evolve over posttreatment intervals. It also interprets its own cross-channel findings against that prior offline-opening evidence, indicating a meaningful framing and analytic role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing study applies Wang and Goldfarb (2017)'s omnichannel empirical lens, especially its dynamic posttreatment-effect specification, to a different intervention: faster online delivery from a new DC. It uses that prior store-opening evidence as a benchmark for how channel effects can vary with existing brand presence.
